scenario: Apex insurer evaluates a Camunda 8.8 DMN table for 4500 claims per day. The architect wants only a single satisfied rule per evaluation across 3 candidate policies because downstream services consume a single output value, not a list of values from multiple rules.
question: Which group of DMN hit policies guarantees a maximum of one satisfied rule per evaluation?

options.text: UNIQUE, ANY, and FIRST always return a maximum of one satisfied rule, according to the role-of-a-hit-policy section of the Camunda 8 DMN docs
UNIQUE, FIRST, and RULE ORDER always return a maximum of one satisfied rule, according to the role-of-a-hit-policy section of the docs
ANY, FIRST, and COLLECT always return a maximum of one satisfied rule, according to the role-of-a-hit-policy section of the docs
UNIQUE, RULE ORDER, and COLLECT always return a maximum of one satisfied rule, according to the role-of-a-hit-policy section of the docs

a.text: Correct. The role-of-a-hit-policy section names UNIQUE, ANY, and FIRST as the three policies that always return a maximum of one satisfied rule in the decision result.

b.text: Incorrect. RULE ORDER is documented as a policy that can return multiple satisfied rules, so it does not belong to the at-most-one group named in the role section of the docs.

c.text: Incorrect. COLLECT is documented as a policy that can return multiple satisfied rules as a list, so it does not belong to the at-most-one group named in the role section.

d.text: Incorrect. Both RULE ORDER and COLLECT are documented as multi-rule policies; only UNIQUE, ANY, and FIRST always return a maximum of one satisfied rule per evaluation.

explanation: The role-of-a-hit-policy section of the Camunda 8 DMN docs splits the supported hit policies into two groups: UNIQUE, ANY, and FIRST always return at most one satisfied rule, while RULE ORDER and COLLECT can return multiple satisfied rules.
